text: As artificial intelligence becomes more common in the workplace, it is changing how we work. One major effect is automation, where AI handles repetitive tasks like data entry or customer service queries. This allows employees to focus on more creative and complex work. Many companies now use productivity tools powered by AI, such as smart calendars or email assistants, which help people manage their time better. However, AI is not simply replacing jobs. Instead, human-AI collaboration is becoming important, with workers using AI as a partner to make decisions or analyse information. This means job roles are changing, requiring new skills like understanding AI outputs and working alongside technology.
